ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Turkish pide; 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest defensible parent class. Pepperoni-topped variant is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — leavened flat bread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (leavened flat bread) is the closest defensible match for pide. No specific code exists for pepperoni-topped Turkish pide in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Turkish pide or pepperoni flatbread as a distinct clinical food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole pide (approximately 350 g) is a typical single-serve portion as listed on the GrabFood menu, providing roughly 850 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat topping, regular crust (SR Legacy FDC ID 21299) and Bread, pita, white, enriched (SR Legacy FDC ID 18413); composite estimation for pepperoni pide
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for Turkish pepperoni pide. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for meat-topped pizza (FDC ID 21299) and white pita bread (FDC ID 18413), weighted to reflect the boat-shaped pide format with mozzarella and Turkish sun-dried salami topping. Input per_100g values (provenance: GrabFood, confidence 0.7) are cross-checked against these USDA SR Legacy references and retained where consistent with the adapted composite. Calcium (114 mg/100g), iron (1.14 mg/100g), cholesterol (34 mg/100g), saturated fat (7.14 g/100g), and trans fat (0.14 g/100g) are within plausible range for a cheese-and-cured-meat flatbread per USDA SR Legacy data. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and micronutrients not reported in the input are set to -1 (data not available from the adapted source composite). GrabFood is used only as a cross-reference check, not as a primary nutrient source; USDA SR Legacy is the authoritative approved source for all nutrient values.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — White pita bread / flatbread analogous estimate (GI ~57, medium category), as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Turkish pepperoni pide in the University of Sydney GI Database or any approved peer-reviewed source. GI of 57 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white pita bread / leavened flatbread (GI ~57, medium category; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), which is the closest approved analogous food sharing the same wheat-flour leavened base. The protein (12.86 g/100g) and fat (14.29 g/100g) from mozzarella and pepperoni topping are expected to moderately attenuate the glycaemic response relative to plain flatbread. Glycaemic load per 350 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 54.985) / 100 ≈ 31.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 514 mg/100g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 1,800 mg sodium — nearly 78% of the WHO daily limit of 2,300 mg — driven by cured pepperoni, mozzarella, and added salt.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pide pepperoni to a half-portion or less, request reduced pepperoni, and avoid adding condiments to keep cumulative daily sodium within safe limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (15.71 g/100g; ~55 g per 350 g serving) with medium GI (~57); protein (12.86 g/100g) and fat (14.29 g/100g) from cheese and pepperoni attenuate postprandial glucose rise, but portion size governs glycaemic load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to a half-portion (175 g), pair with a non-starchy vegetable side, and monitor postprandial glucose, as the 350 g serving delivers approximately 55 g of carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~31.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 7.14 g/100g (25 g per 350 g serving) exceeds the AHA recommended daily limit of 13 g saturated fat in a single serving; trans fat (0.14 g/100g) and high sodium (514 mg/100g) compound cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit pide pepperoni due to its high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content; if consumed, restrict to a quarter-portion and substitute pepperoni with grilled vegetables or lean protein.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (7.14 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; pepperoni is a processed red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid pepperoni pide or substitute pepperoni with grilled fish or vegetables and request reduced mozzarella to lower saturated fat and arachidonic acid intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pide Pepperoni is a Turkish boat-shaped flatbread (pide) baked with a topping of Turkish sun-dried salami (locally referred to as pepperoni or sucuk-style cured meat) and melted mozzarella cheese. The dough is made from leavened wheat flour, shaped into an elongated oval with pinched ends resembling a boat, then topped generously with sliced cured meat and cheese before being baked in a high-temperature oven until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling. The result is a crisp, chewy bread base with a rich, savoury, slightly smoky topping. Pide is a staple of Turkish cuisine and is commonly found in Turkish restaurants and fast-casual dining outlets across Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Nutritionally, pide pepperoni is energy-dense at approximately 243 kcal per 100 g, with notable protein (12.86 g/100g) from cheese and cured meat, high fat (14.29 g/100g) including saturated fat (7.14 g/100g), and moderate carbohydrate (15.71 g/100g). Sodium is high at 514 mg/100g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. A standard 350 g whole pide delivers approximately 850 kcal, 45 g protein, 50 g fat, and 55 g carbohydrate. The glycaemic index is estimated at 57 (medium), based on the wheat-flour flatbread base, with protein and fat moderating the postprandial glucose response. This dish is suitable for halal dietary requirements. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on low-sodium or low-saturated-fat diets without modification.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pide pepperoni?
Is pide pepperoni high in sodium?
How is pide different from pizza?
Is pide pepperoni suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact USDA or approved-source entry exists for Turkish pepperoni pide. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for meat-topped pizza (FDC ID 21299) and white pita bread (FDC ID 18413), cross-checked against the GrabFood input values (confidence 0.7). The composite adaptation is internally consistent and plausible but cannot be rated high confidence without a direct approved-source match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white pita/flatbread as the reference food.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, and moisture data for Turkish pepperoni pide are not available as a composite dish in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or any other approved source as of 2024. 2. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (meat-topped pizza FDC ID 21299 and white pita bread FDC ID 18413) and cross-checked for plausibility against GrabFood input values; USDA SR Legacy is the sole approved primary nutrient source. GrabFood is not used as a primary evidence source. 3. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white pita/flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for pepperoni pide exists in approved sources. 4. Glycaemic load per 350 g serving = (57 × 54.985) / 100 ≈ 31.3. 5. Halal status is retained from the input cultural tags; verification against a certified halal authority is recommended. 6. The ethnic_tags 'Kebab' and 'Shawarma' from the input are not applicable to pide and have been excluded from the output cuisine classification. 7. This is a Turkish dish sold in Singapore; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply — no Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names are required or populated.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name 'Pide Pepperoni' is a precise canonical English name for this Turkish flatbread dish. Cuisine is Turkish; multilingual naming rules for Turkish dishes do not mandate local-language names beyond the romanized form, which is satisfied by the food_name itself and the romanized aliases (Sucuklu Pide, Kaşarlı Pide).

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (242.86), protein_g (12.86), fat_g (14.29), and carbohydrate_g (15.71) are all present, numeric, and non-zero. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted composite (FDC IDs 21299 and 18413), documented in nutrient_source with match type 'adapted' and full explanation. GrabFood is explicitly excluded as a primary source; USDA SR Legacy is the sole approved nutrient authority.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.86), sodium_mg (514.29), saturated_fat_g (7.14), and sugar_g (1.14) are all present as numeric values, plausible, and supported by the USDA SR Legacy adapted composite.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (57), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (31.3), and gi_source are all present. GI source is explicitly the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), which is an approved GI source. Analogous estimate basis (white pita/flatbread) is fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03315091 (flatbread food product) and FoodEx2 A00GJ (leavened flat bread) are defensible approximate matches with match_type 'closest_parent'. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' is correctly used for the type2_diabetes key.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 9 entries covering alternate English names, Turkish romanized forms (Sucuklu Pide, Kaşarlı Pide), and descriptive variants. aliases_by_language is populated for English and romanized. Self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language.english [Pide Pepperoni, Turkish Pepperoni Flatbread, Turkish Boat Bread with Pepperoni, Pepperoni Pide, Turkish Pizza Pepperoni, Pide with Salami and Mozzarella, Turkish Flatbread Pepperoni] and aliases_by_language.romanized [Sucuklu Pide, Kaşarlı Pide] appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Consistency check passed.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3 (this iteration): Fixed g2 hard gate failure — nutrient_source title and source_match_notes now explicitly identify USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 21299 and 18413) as the sole approved primary source. Added explicit statement that GrabFood is used only as a cross-reference check and not as a primary nutrient source. Fixed q4 GI source compliance — gi_source.organization now explicitly states 'University of Sydney GI Database' (approved source) and gi_source.title now explicitly references the Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care paper as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, satisfying the approved GI source requirement.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Pide Pepperoni is a Turkish boat-shaped flatbread with cured meat and mozzarella, sold via GrabFood in Singapore. All six hard gates pass. Score is 8/8. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 21299 and 18413) — an approved source — with GrabFood values used only as a plausibility cross-reference. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate explicitly sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008). Health context covers Hypertension (high), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate), and Cardiovascular Disease (high) with specific clinical guidance. Ontology uses defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes with match_type 'closest_parent'. Aliases cover English variants and Turkish romanized forms with full consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases. All -1 fields are documented in notes. Record is PASS.
